nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Enforce `readyForDispatch` in the dispatch flow**

This commit computes `readyForDispatch`, but the new delta-review model is not consumed by runtime flow control (repo-wide references to `buildDiffFirstDeltaReview`/`readyForDispatch` are only in `tests/s18/delta_review.test.ts`), so a no-op delta is still not actually blocked before dispatch in production paths. In the exact scenario this change targets (prior/current artifacts identical), the code can still proceed through existing state transitions because no transition guard reads this flag.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Escape flattened prompt paths to avoid collisions**

The flattener builds paths with raw `.`/`[index]` delimiters, so object keys containing those characters (for example `"a.b"` or `"items[0]"`) collide with nested/array paths and can hide real structural changes. With such templates, `diffPrompt` can incorrectly return no entries and mark a material delta as unchanged, which then drives incorrect review and dispatch decisions.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
